title: 23. MuseTalk Preprocess
description: Prepare MuseTalk avatar packs, source media, emotion tags, frame cache, mask ranges, and first-frame debug output.

steps.title: Open MuseTalk Preprocess
steps.body: Preprocess is the MuseTalk avatar preparation tool. Use it before selecting an avatar pack in the main Host settings. Use Read steps aloud or Read Step in this tutorial panel if you want spoken guidance while you work.

steps.title: Choose the Target Pack
steps.body: The target pack is where prepared avatars and emotion variants are stored. Create or refresh the pack list before importing new source media.

steps.title: Choose Source Media
steps.body: Source can be a video, still image, or folder of frames. Use clear source names so generated avatar IDs and emotion variants stay understandable later.

steps.title: Name the Avatar Variant
steps.body: Avatar ID is the prepared variant name. Use simple lowercase names such as neutral, angry, shy, or surprised so persona emotion tags can match them.

steps.title: Prepare and Cache Frames
steps.body: Prepare Avatar runs the preprocessing path. Create frame cache makes startup faster later, at the cost of more disk space.

steps.title: Debug the First Frame
steps.body: First-frame debug helps you inspect bbox shift, parsing mode, mask overlays, and cheek/margin settings before running a full avatar preprocess.

steps.title: Save Metadata
steps.body: Save metadata after emotion tags, default avatar choice, and mask tweaks are correct. This is what later avatar selection depends on.
